mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly tracks nominal consumer expenditure, which is the largest component of aggregate demand; sustained growth in this series drives positive expected S&P 500 forward 20-day returns through revenue channel effects.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or top-decile RSAFS growth periods do not yield positive average 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,100 thousand units (HOUST > 1100) signals a durable residential investment recovery that propagates into construction employment and durable goods demand, lifting cyclical S&P 500 sectors and generating positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1100, average 60-day forward returns are not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month changes in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) are both positive, their interaction captures a coordinated expansion in output and labor income that is not fully priced by either series alone, predicting positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns beyond the sum of individual effects.
failure_condition: In a regression of 20-day forward returns on INDPRO and PAYEMS, the interaction term does not carry a positive coefficient significant at the 5% level over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%), rising year-over-year consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals successful reflation that erodes real debt burdens and boosts nominal corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 20-day returns; this relationship inverts during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, rising CPIAUCSL does not predict positive 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and a low-rates dummy is not positive and significant at 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a rolling 3-month window triggers negative 5-day S&P 500 returns because labor market deterioration signals incoming demand shortfalls and rising credit risk.
failure_condition: Following trigger activation, the proportion of negative 5-day return instances falls below 55% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 0.2% triggers negative 5-day S&P 500 returns by compressing real disposable income and raising nominal discount rate expectations before the Federal Reserve responds.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward returns following the trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) tracks goods sector profitability and capacity utilization; persistent expansion predicts positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns through operating leverage and positive earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Top-quintile INDPRO growth months do not produce statistically significant positive average 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 50,000 workers triggers negative 60-day S&P 500 returns because abrupt job losses signal recession onset and collapse in aggregate household income.
failure_condition: Following trigger activation, average 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.
